architecture.summary: Tyler is a typed latent reasoning framework that extends autoregressive decoding by allowing an LLM to interleave between emitting visible tokens and invoking three specialized latent operators. During each decoding step, the backbone LLM's policy chooses whether to emit a visible token or invoke one of three typed operators (O_plan for global orientation, O_update for local state updates, O_proc for procedural abstraction). When an operator is invoked, a LoRA-augmented latent synthesizer maps the current decoding context to operator-conditioned latent tokens, which are inserted back into the context to guide subsequent visible decoding. The framework is trained in two stages: Stage 1 optimizes the latent synthesizer while keeping the LLM frozen, aligning operators with their intended roles via structure-aware heuristics; Stage 2 learns a budget-aware invocation policy via GRPO while keeping synthesis parameters frozen.

biblio.title: Tyler: Typed Latent Reasoning for Language Models -- When to Think, What to Compute, and How Much to Allocate

claims.dataset: macro-average across GSM8K, MATH-500, GPQA-Diamond, TheoremQA (SmolLM3-3B)
claims.statement: Tyler Stage 2 improves accuracy by up to 14.49 points over CoT

claims.dataset: macro-average across GSM8K, MATH-500, GPQA-Diamond, TheoremQA (SmolLM3-3B)
claims.statement: Tyler Stage 2 improves accuracy by up to 4.30 points over the strongest competing baseline

claims.dataset: macro-average across GSM8K, MATH-500, GPQA-Diamond, TheoremQA
claims.statement: On SmolLM3-3B, Tyler Stage 2 reaches an average accuracy of 56.03%, outperforming CoT by 14.49 points and MemGen by 4.30 points

claims.dataset: macro-average across GSM8K, MATH-500, GPQA-Diamond, TheoremQA
claims.statement: On Qwen3-4B, Tyler Stage 2 reaches 65.78%, improving over CoT by 9.22 points and over GRPO by 3.13 points

In continual learning across code, science, math, and theorem reasoning, Tyler achieves the highest macro-average accuracy and the lowest forgetting score, reducing forgetting by about 1.46 points compared with MemGen
Tyler generalizes across diverse reasoning domains: gains on mathematical reasoning (GSM8K, MATH-500) transfer to scientific reasoning (GPQA-Diamond) and theorem-oriented reasoning (TheoremQA)

The three typed operators induce distinct functional roles: O_plan leads to more strategy and setup errors when removed, O_update increases state and arithmetic errors, and O_proc produces more procedural and symbolic errors

Tyler exhibits task-adaptive invocation: on simpler tasks (GSM8K) the invocation ratio remains low, while on harder tasks (GPQA-Diamond) latent operators are triggered more frequently, and O_update is invoked more in early-middle stages while O_proc dominates later stages
